questions.question: A bag contains 3 red marbles and 7 blue marbles. If you draw one marble at random, what is the probability that it is red?
questions.explanation: There are 3 red marbles out of 10 total marbles. Probability = favorable outcomes / total outcomes = 3/10.

questions.question: A coin is flipped twice. What is the probability of getting heads on the first flip and tails on the second flip?
questions.explanation: Each flip is independent. P(heads on first) = 1/2, P(tails on second) = 1/2. Multiply: (1/2) × (1/2) = 1/4.

questions.question: In a class, 12 students play soccer and 8 students play basketball. No student plays both sports. If there are 25 students total, what is the probability that a randomly selected student plays neither sport?
questions.explanation: Students playing at least one sport: 12 + 8 = 20. Students playing neither: 25 − 20 = 5. Probability = 5/25 = 1/5.

questions.question: A two-way table shows that out of 100 people surveyed, 60 own a car and 40 do not. Of those who own a car, 45 have a driver's license. What is the probability that a randomly selected person owns a car AND has a driver's license?
questions.explanation: 45 people own a car and have a license out of 100 total. Probability = 45/100 = 9/20.

questions.question: A spinner has 5 equal sections labeled 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5. You spin twice. What is the probability that both spins show an odd number?
questions.explanation: Odd numbers are 1, 3, 5 (three out of five sections). P(odd on first) = 3/5, P(odd on second) = 3/5. Multiply: (3/5) × (3/5) = 9/25.

questions.question: A deck contains 52 cards: 13 hearts, 13 diamonds, 13 clubs, and 13 spades. You draw one card. What is the probability it is not a heart?
